2603.25723.title: Natural-Language Agent Harnesses
2603.25723.paper_summary: Agent performance increasingly depends on \emph{harness engineering}, yet harness design is usually buried in controller code and runtime-specific conventions, making it hard to transfer, compare, and study as a scientific object. We ask whether the high-level control logic of an agent harness can instead be externalized as a portable executable artifact. We introduce \textbf{Natural-Language Agent Harnesses} (NLAHs), which express harness behavior in editable natural language, and \textbf{Intelligent Harness Runtime} (IHR), a shared runtime that executes these harnesses through explicit contracts, durable artifacts, and lightweight adapters. Across coding and computer-use benchmarks, we conduct controlled evaluations of operational viability, module ablation, and code-to-text harness migration.
2603.25723.paper_summary_zh: 代理性能越來越依賴於 \emph{鞍具工程}，然而鞍具設計通常埋藏在控制器代碼和運行時特定的約定中，使得它難以作為科學對象進行轉移、比較和研究。我們詢問是否可以將代理鞍具的高級控制邏輯外部化為可攜帶的可執行工件。我們介紹了 \textbf{自然語言代理鞍具} (NLAHs)，它以可編輯的自然語言表達鞍具行為，以及 \textbf{智能鞍具運行時} (IHR)，這是一個通過明確的合約、持久的工件和輕量級適配器執行這些鞍具的共享運行時。在編碼和計算機使用基準測試中，我們進行了操作可行性、模塊消融和代碼到文本鞍具遷移的控制評估。
2603.25723.author: Linyue Pan et.al.
2603.25723.authors: Linyue Pan, Lexiao Zou, Shuo Guo, Jingchen Ni, Hai-Tao Zheng
